summary:
The primary mediator for ganglionic transmission involves binding of acetylcholine to nicotine-sensitive receptors causing neuronal activation through a process known as depolarization. Key findings from multiple studies confirm this mechanism accurately describes how signals are transmitted at neuromuscular junctions involving autonomic nervous system activity.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two sentences focusing only on key clinical points related to the question about ganglionic transmission mediation:

Ganglionic transmission primarily occurs via interaction between acetylcholine and nicotinic receptors resulting in neuron depolarization. Studies uniformly support this role of cholinergic signaling in transmitting nerve impulses across synapses involved in both parasympathetic and sympathetic pathways. 

The summary captures the essential elements without extraneous details while addressing core aspects needed clinically regarding what mediates ganglionic transmissions physiologically. Let me know if you need any other adjustments!
